A young man who loves hip-hop above all else. He has a scary look in his eyes and isn't friendly, so people find him hard to talk to, but he's actually just awkward. He was born a quarter-Russian to a pianist father and an opera singer mother. He is also from a well-known musical family, but he left home after his parents disapproved of his passion for hip-hop.[1]

Allen is a Eurasian male with a slender physique and dark red hair and piercing red eyes. He wears a black t-shirt with a red hoodie on top of it. Allen also wears a matching set of light blue denim jacket and pants embellished with flame patches, orange-embroidered on red felt. He also wears red denim sneakers, black fingerless gloves with pyramid studs, and a red ring on his right thumb. Allen has a flame tattoo on the right side of his neck and wears two chain necklaces; one plain and one with a phoenix and cross on it. He also wears an ear cuff on the left side of his ear.

In his Exhibition Show outfit, Allen wears a white utility jacket with light blue trim and red buttons and pockets. There are orange straps hanging from the shoulders and straps from the middle of the jacket that repeat the team name "BAE" on them. The collar has a cord lock and an orange belt. The sleeves have an orange stripe down the side and red cuffs with a black stripe. Beneath the jacket, Allen wears a black, oversized scoop neck t-shirt with a small, light blue tag with a black fiery logo at the hem. He also wears loose-fit red shorts with pockets and a black trim, and black leggings with a red fiery pattern beneath them. His sneakers are light blue with a black tongue and red laces and soles.

Personality

Due to being awkward and often looking unfriendly, Allen is bad in social situations and can struggles to communicate his words properly. In truth, Allen is a very earnest, honest and frank person, though this causes him to rather blunt and tactless at times. He tends to be headstrong, emotional, and rather tends to do things his own way, he still cares a lot about his friends and listen to them when it counts. He has deep bond with Hajun and Anne, and treasures them greatly, getting upset on their behalf, defends them, and leads them with grace.[2][3][4]

As a "hip-hop maniac", Allen is extremely passionate and knowledgeable about the culture and its musical history. Whenever hiptop topics comes up, Allen becomes so talkative to the point other people get uncomfortable and overwhelmed by his enthusia
